items.title: Hayom Yom / daily Chitas (Chabad)
items.explanation: Chabad custom: daily Chumash, Tehillim, and Tanya (Chitas), plus Hayom Yom. Many complete it after morning prayers, but you may learn today's portion anytime before the next Hebrew day — including at night if you did not finish earlier.

items.title: Korbanot before Pesukei Dezimra (Ashkenaz)
items.explanation: Ashkenaz siddurim include Korbanot (Temple offerings) before Pesukei Dezimra. Many say at least Parshat HaTamid; follow your siddur and rabbi.

items.title: Korbanot & preliminaries (Nusach Ari)
items.explanation: Tehillat Hashem includes Korbanot and other passages before Pesukei Dezimra. Follow the order in your Chabad siddur.

items.title: Birkat Kohanim in Shacharit (Sephardi / Edot HaMizrach)
items.explanation: Sephardi and Edot HaMizrach communities in the Diaspora commonly recite Birkat Kohanim daily during Shacharit (customs vary — follow your kehilla and Shulchan Aruch O.C. 128).

items.title: Korbanot before Mincha (Sephardi)
items.explanation: Sephardi siddurim open Mincha with Korbanot — especially Parshat HaTamid (the daily Temple offering) — before Ashrei and the Amidah (Shulchan Arukh O.C. 1:5, 232:1). Follow your siddur and kehilla.

items.title: Korbanot before Mincha (Edot HaMizrach)
items.explanation: Edot HaMizrach siddurim open Mincha with Korbanot — especially Parshat HaTamid — before Ashrei and the Amidah, matching the Shulchan Arukh order (O.C. 1:5, 232:1). Some kehillot include additional passages; follow your siddur.

items.title: Tachanun at Mincha (Ashkenaz weekdays)
items.explanation:
Tachanun at Shacharit (weekdays): After the morning Amidah — longer on Mondays and Thursdays (Vehu Rachum and the full penitential section), shorter on other weekdays. Omitted on Rosh Chodesh, festivals, Chanukah, and other days your siddur lists.

Tachanun at Mincha: Some Ashkenaz communities especially emphasize saying Tachanun at Mincha if it was skipped at Shacharit that day. On a normal weekday, Tachanun at Mincha is already part of the standard service (often the shorter form). Omitted on the same festive days — Mincha itself is still said.
